name: Miller (Multi-Purpose Data Tool)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 2-Clause); John Kerl; rewritten in Go (was C)

performance.notes: Go implementation; fast streaming; handles large files efficiently with constant memory

agent_readiness.notes: Swiss army knife for CSV, TSV, JSON, NDJSON, DKVP (key=value), and PPRINT data. Built-in verb language (mlr) for field manipulation, filter, sort, stats, reshape, join. Handles heterogeneous data (different fields per record). name-indexed like awk but with data-type awareness. Streaming: processes files larger than RAM. Auto-detects format. `mlr --csv cut -f name,age data.csv`. Can output to different format than input. Widely used in data engineering and sysops.

gotchas: DSL syntax has learning curve (not standard SQL or shell)
Verb chaining can get complex for multi-step transformations
Less convenient than pandas for complex reshaping
Documentation is extensive but can be overwhelming for beginners
best_when: Streaming large CSV/JSON/TSV files without loading into memory
Format conversion (CSV → JSON, NDJSON → CSV, etc.)
Quick stats, filter, sort in Unix pipelines
avoid_when: Complex joins across many files (DuckDB or pandas better)
Teams unfamiliar with mlr DSL syntax
When SQL queries are the natural fit
summary: Miller (mlr) is a streaming multi-format data processing tool for CSV, TSV, JSON, NDJSON, and DKVP. Built-in verb DSL for filter, sort, stats, reshape, and join. Processes files larger than RAM via streaming. Auto-detects input format, converts to any output format. Faster than Python alternatives. Swiss army knife for data engineering pipelines and sysops. v6 rewritten in Go from C.
